description: memx built-in benchmark: 30 queries across 50 memories, covering exact keywords, semantic recall, CJK, entity relationships, and cross-category retrieval.

memories.content: acme-backend unit test initiative: 234 tests passing across 9 service packages. Tests use JUnit 4 + Mockito 5.x.
ACME-3078 blocked by upstream dependency on payment-service v2.4. ETA unblock: 2026-03-15.
devtools AI Code Slack report feature shipped 2026-04-08. Daily report at 21:00 UTC+8, weekly on Fridays.
memxcore P0 compact auto-recovery and P1 BM25 hybrid search completed 2026-04-09.
Q2 OKR: O1=Developer productivity (40%), O2=Platform reliability (40%), O3=Support requests (20%).
Confluence weekly report page_id=123456789. Workflow: read git log + tickets, map to OKR, update via storage format.
Jira MCP configured. Tool: uvx mcp-atlassian. Jira domain: acme.atlassian.net.
Front-end deploy rule: acme-frontend only counts MRs merged to develop branch (hardcoded).
Confluence update_page API must use content_format='storage' (XHTML), not Markdown. Tables break with Markdown format.
Scheduled task design: tick-based (check every minute) is better than sleep-based when DB dedup exists. Sleep is not robust against NTP jumps.
Git commit message conventions: use imperative mood, reference ticket IDs, keep under 72 chars for first line.
ChromaDB cosine similarity: score = 1.0 - distance. Higher is more similar. Collection metadata: hnsw:space=cosine.
BM25 tokenization for CJK: each character is one token. English words split by whitespace. No need for jieba at small corpus scale.
RRF fusion formula: score(d) = 1/(k+rank_rag) + 1/(k+rank_bm25), k=60. Documents in both rankers get boosted.
Python asyncio.gather vs threading.Thread: use threading for CPU-bound with GIL release (e.g., ChromaDB), asyncio for I/O-bound.
SQLite WAL mode enables concurrent readers with one writer. Default journal mode blocks readers during writes.
User is a team lead responsible for developer tooling and platform engineering.
User has strong product thinking: can extrapolate from technical infrastructure to business models.
User prefers Chinese communication, short and direct. Technical terms should not be forcefully translated to Chinese.
User explicitly separates company work from personal projects. Company reports must never include personal project content.
User drills down through design quality layer by layer: surface issue, restart edge case, dedup, time jitter, cross-weekend bug, message wording.
2026-04-08: Discovered that author name dedup requires email-based key, not name-based. Two 'Richard' entries had same email.
2026-04-07: memx initial release. Core complete: remember, search, compact, reindex. MCP + CLI working.
2026-03-20: Production incident — payment-service returned 500 errors for 2 hours due to connection pool exhaustion. Root cause: missing connection timeout.
2026-04-09: MemPalace comparison analysis completed. Key insight: knowledge graph and search layering are the biggest gaps in memx.
Grafana oncall dashboard: grafana.internal/d/api-latency. Check when editing request-path code.
Pipeline bugs tracked in Linear project 'INGEST'. Use Linear search for pipeline-related issues.
memxcore repo: https://github.com/Danny0218/memX. README.md has full setup guide.
LongMemEval benchmark paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.10813. 500 questions testing long-term memory retrieval.
AI coding keywords for commit detection: cursor, claude, codex in commit message, MR title, or description.
Alice joined the frontend team on 2026-03-01. She took over acme-frontend from Bob.
Sprint 47 goal: complete auth middleware rewrite. Driven by legal compliance, not tech debt.
JWT vs session tokens: JWT is stateless but can't be revoked without a blocklist. Session tokens allow server-side revocation.
2026-02-15: Decided to use Redis for session storage over Memcached. Reason: Redis supports TTL per key and pub/sub for invalidation.
Docker multi-stage build: use builder stage for compilation, copy only artifacts to runtime stage. Reduces image size by 60-80%.
Maya is the DevOps lead. She manages the CI/CD pipeline and Kubernetes clusters.
2026-03-10: Database migration 0042_user_schema added NOT NULL column to users table (50M rows). Used batched backfill to avoid long locks.
Kubernetes HPA: scale on CPU > 70% with min 2, max 10 replicas. Use custom metrics for queue-based scaling.
Team Slack channel: #platform-eng. Daily standup at 10:00 UTC+8.
Performance budget: API p99 latency must stay under 200ms. Current p99 is 145ms.
PostgreSQL EXPLAIN ANALYZE: look for Seq Scan on large tables, Nested Loop with high row estimates, and Sort operations without index.
2026-01-20: Kai left the Orion project. Handoff to Chen completed. All docs in Confluence space ORION.
Orion project sunset planned for 2026-06-30. Migration to new platform starts 2026-05-01.
Rate limiting strategies: token bucket for burst tolerance, sliding window for smooth enforcement. Use Redis sorted sets for distributed sliding window.
Architecture decision records (ADR) in Confluence space ARCH. Template: context, decision, consequences.
2026-04-01: Switched from Datadog to Grafana for cost savings. Annual savings estimated at $15K.
OpenTelemetry: use OTLP exporter for traces and metrics. Collector runs as sidecar in k8s. Supports both push and pull.
Tech debt backlog: 47 items. Top 3: auth middleware (legal), connection pool tuning (incident), test coverage gap (quality).
User requires AI to proactively propose complete solutions, not wait for step-by-step guidance. Anti-pattern: asking 4 rounds to reach a solution that should take 1.
Three-question rule before proposing solutions: 1) What will user ask after seeing result? 2) Are there related issues I noticed but didn't mention? 3) Am I making user do judgments I should make?

queries.expected_content: team lead responsible for developer tooling
